sessions.intent: Define failing-test contract for moving per-action LLM teaching from C# attributes/Compile.llm to markdown files at os/system/modules/<module>/.

before.assumptions: Architect plan in .bot/compile-llm-notes-per-action/architect/plan.md is approved. Catalog entry (app.goals.goal.steps.step.actions.action.@this) already exposes Description/ModuleDescription/Examples — coder will add Notes/ModuleNotes (action-level + module-level), populate Description/ModuleDescription/Examples from markdown after migration script runs, and surface text fields per the merge-at-render rule. Provider→Code attribute rename already in source.
before.risk: Drift cases are LLM-sensitive; assertions must accept the structural shape (no spurious channel/Message) without overfitting to literal prompt text. Loader/renderer tests reference a markdown-fixture filesystem; need to fit existing MockFileSystem patterns rather than touching real os/system/modules/. Modifier folder rename (AiModule/ → ai/) is part of branch but I am NOT testing rename — only the loader behavior on the new lowercase paths.

actions.context: Drift cases — the load-bearing pair from the architect's brief. Plang `--test` goals; bodies are `throw "not implemented"`. Commentary pins the pre-fix shape that is forbidden and the architect's 3-fresh-cache-builds rule.

Mechanism tests for the markdown teaching loader: per-layer reads (module vs action), merge order (module first, blank line, action), and orphan-file warnings. New folder `CatalogTests/` per the project alias-clash convention (PLang.Tests/GlobalUsings.cs aliases `Data`). TUnit `[Test]` + `Assert.Fail("Not implemented")`.

Renderer tests: per-action blocks only render for planner's set; modifier actions render via same path; system-prompt size on a Tests/Simple step compile drops below 16 KB (architect verification check #1).
Provider→Code rename guard. Source already shows [Code] everywhere — guards against the old name reappearing and pins that PLNG001 diagnostic text teaches [Code].

after.health: All test files written failing-by-construction per character contract. Two plang drift tests + 22 C# tests across 5 files.
after.notes: User skipped per-batch approval. Coder is next; the two drift cases are the load-bearing tests — verify across 3 fresh-cache builds (CLAUDE.md "Stale-binary trap").
